command: mysql --batch --raw --column-names --default-character-set=utf8mb4 --host gateway03.us-east-1.prod.aws.tidbcloud.com --port 4000 --user 39grZh2mCwvwFak.407bcbcd1378 --database j8UWnWEhQvEwUyDXxqYhUh --execute SELECT * FROM courses LIMIT 5;

rows.title: Neuromorphic Brain-Computer Interfaces: Principles and Applications
rows.description: This comprehensive course delves into the cutting-edge field of Neuromorphic Brain-Computer Interfaces (BCIs), exploring how brain-inspired computing architectures can revolutionize the way we interact with technology. Participants will gain a deep understanding of neuromorphic engineering principles, BCI fundamentals, and the unique challenges and opportunities presented by integrating these two disciplines. The course covers theoretical foundations, hardware implementations, data processing techniques, and real-world applications, preparing learners to contribute to the next generation of intelligent neuroprosthetics and human-machine interaction systems.

rows.title: Gnosticism in Modern Esoteric Culture: From Ancient Heresy to Contemporary Spirituality
rows.description: This advanced course provides an academically rigorous exploration of Gnosticism, tracing its historical roots from ancient antiquity through its complex reception and reinterpretation within modern esoteric traditions. We will critically analyze core Gnostic concepts, examine primary texts, and investigate how Gnostic themes have influenced and been transformed by movements such as Theosophy, Jungian psychology, Western ceremonial magic, and contemporary New Age spirituality. The course emphasizes critical analysis, historical contextualization, and the synthesis of complex ideas, assuming a solid foundational knowledge of religious studies and Western esotericism.

rows.title: Advanced Cognitive Neuroprosthetics: Bridging Mind and Machine
rows.description: This advanced course delves into the theoretical underpinnings, technological advancements, and ethical considerations of cognitive neuroprosthetics. Designed for students with a solid foundation in neuroscience, engineering, and cognitive science, it explores how neural interfaces can restore, augment, or replace cognitive functions, moving beyond motor control to address higher-order processes like memory, decision-making, and communication. The course emphasizes critical analysis of current research, future directions, and the profound societal implications of these transformative technologies.

rows.title: Epsilon-Near-Zero Metamaterials: Fundamentals, Design, and Applications
rows.description: This course provides an expert-level exploration of Epsilon-Near-Zero (ENZ) metamaterials, delving into their unique electromagnetic properties, theoretical underpinnings, and diverse practical applications. Learners will gain a deep understanding of ENZ physics, design principles, fabrication techniques, and their transformative potential in areas like light manipulation, sensing, and energy harvesting. The course balances rigorous theory with real-world examples and critical analysis.

rows.title: Quantum Entanglement-Enhanced Novel Sensing (QUEENS) DARPA Initiative
rows.description: This comprehensive course explores the DARPA QUEENS program, delving into the theoretical foundations of quantum entanglement and its cutting-edge applications in sensing. Learners will gain a deep understanding of how quantum mechanics revolutionizes measurement precision, imaging, and navigation, alongside practical considerations for implementing these advanced technologies. The course balances fundamental principles with real-world applications and future prospects.
